name: examples
description: Jupyter Notebooks to help you get hands-on with Pinecone vector databases

commit.message: Do not commit secrets in .env

docs: add AI agents and update development workflow

Add specialized AI agents for common repository tasks:
- notebook-reviewer: Review notebooks against quality standards
- notebook-creator: Create notebooks following best practices
- pinecone-tester: Test Pinecone integrations and SDK usage
- docs-updater: Update documentation and maintain consistency
- example-migrator: Migrate examples to newer SDK versions

Agents are available for both Claude Code (.claude/agents/) and
Cursor (.cursor/commands/) with identical functionality.

Also update CONTRIBUTING.md to:
- Use uv instead of poetry for package management
- Document how to work with AI agents
- Reference new .ai/ standards documentation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: create .ai/ canonical docs and eliminate duplication

Creates single source of truth for notebook standards and writing
guidelines, eliminating content duplication across rules, skills,
commands, and templates.

Changes:
- Add .ai/ directory with canonical documentation:
  - notebook-standards.md: Complete technical requirements
  - writing-guidelines.md: Comprehensive writing guidance
  - quality-checklist.md: Quick reference checklist
- Slim down rules from ~120 to ~40 lines (67% reduction)
  - Rules now reference .ai/ docs for details
  - Maintain high-level principles only
- Update commands to reference skills for quality review
- Remove Claude Code attribution from PR templates
- Delete .github/NOTEBOOK_REVIEW_TEMPLATE.md (eliminated 4x duplication)
- Fix voice guidance conflicts (we/you/impersonal)
- Align dependency pinning policy across all layers

Benefits:
- Single source of truth (update once, referenced everywhere)
- Zero duplication (was 3-4x, now 1x)
- 67% less always-on context in rules
- Clear hierarchy: .ai/ (WHAT) → Rules (principles) → Skills (HOW)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Ignore worktree directory to prevent tracking worktree contents.

commit.message:
fix(ticketbot): use detached HEAD for PR checkout

Adds --detach flag to gh pr checkout to allow multiple worktrees
to work on the same PR without branch conflicts.
chore: ignore egg-info directory

refactor(ticketbot): reduce iterate worker sleep to 10s

Pick workers still use the full interval between iterations to control
ticket creation rate. Iterate workers now only pause 10 seconds between
iterations to avoid rate limits while staying responsive.

fix(deps): align Python version requirements to >=3.10

- Added PEP 621 [project] section for uv compatibility
- Updated requires-python to >=3.10,<3.14 (click>=8.3 requires Python 3.10+)
- Added dev dependencies in PEP 621 format
- Regenerated uv.lock with correct Python constraints

fix(deps): remove conflicting click version constraints

Removed duplicate [dependency-groups] section added by uv and
aligned poetry constraint to ^8.3 to match installed version.

fix(commands): make iterate command more autonomous

- Do not ask which PR to work on - pick first one
- Do not wait for confirmation
- Emphasize immediate action throughout

fix(commands): prevent ticketbot files from being staged in PRs

- Reset ticketbot files after PR checkout
- Explicit instructions to stage only specific files, not git add -A
- Warn against staging automation files

refactor(scripts): merge iterate and merge workers into one

The iterate worker now also handles merging when PR is ready:
- After addressing feedback, checks if PR meets merge conditions
- If ready, merges and marks ticket as Done
- Removes separate merge worker pool for simpler architecture

feat(scripts): add tb-cleanup-orphaned command

Finds tickets marked "In Progress" that have no associated GitHub PR
and moves them back to Backlog. Also handles tickets with merged PRs
by marking them as Done.
chore(scripts): reduce default interval from 120s to 45s

fix(commands): make tb-iterate-review-tickets more action-oriented

Clarify that the agent should pick ONE ticket and actively work on it,
not just list PRs. Added explicit bash commands for checkout and checks.

feat(scripts): add graceful drain mode for ticketbot shutdown

When Ctrl+C is pressed:
- Workers finish their current iteration before exiting
- Parent waits up to 10 minutes for workers to drain
- Force kills only if timeout exceeded
fix(scripts): add --force and --approve-mcps flags for headless agent

feat(scripts): add ticketbot for automated ticket processing

Adds a Python CLI script with independent worker pools to manage
the ticket lifecycle: picking work, iterating on PRs, and merging.
Each worker runs in its own git worktree to avoid conflicts.

- ticketbot.py: Main CLI with start-all, run, and test commands
- tb-pick-next-ticket.md: Check capacity, pick backlog ticket, implement
- tb-iterate-review-tickets.md: Find in-review tickets, iterate on PRs
- tb-merge-ready-prs.md: Find mergeable PRs, verify and merge

fix(learn): modernize merge-namespaces.ipynb to SDK v8 (#532)

## Summary

- Update pinecone-client[grpc] to pinecone[grpc]==8.0.0
- Consolidate imports into first code cell with try/except wrapper
- Remove duplicate imports from later cells

## Audience and Use Case

This notebook demonstrates how to merge vectors from two namespaces into
a single namespace in a Pinecone index.

## Prerequisites

- Pinecone API key
- An existing Pinecone index with data in multiple namespaces

## Key Concepts

- Fetching all vectors from a namespace using query
- Reformatting fetched vectors for upsert
- Batched upsert operations

## Links

- Linear: SDK-223

commit.message:
fix(docs): update onboarding-recommender.ipynb to SDK v8 (#531)

## Summary

- Update pinecone-client==3.1.0 to pinecone==8.0.0
- Consolidate imports into first code cell with try/except wrapper
- Remove duplicate imports from later cells
- Add cleanup cell to delete index at end
- Fix broken link (docs.pinecone.io/docs/projects →
guides/projects/understanding-projects)
- Fix notebook JSON structure

## Audience and Use Case

This notebook demonstrates how to build a movie recommendation system
using Pinecone. It shows how to use pre-embedded datasets, configure a
serverless index, and query for similar items based on user preferences.

## Prerequisites

- Pinecone API key (free tier available)

## Key Concepts

- Recommendation systems using vector similarity
- Pre-embedded datasets from pinecone-datasets
- HuggingFace embedding models for users and items
- Serverless index configuration

## Links

- Linear: SDK-133

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Docs-only changes, but it updates example code to a new major SDK
version and changes index lifecycle calls, which could break if the
notebook isn’t re-run and validated.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates `docs/onboarding-recommender.ipynb` to use Pinecone Python SDK
v8 (`pinecone==8.0.0`) and the new client APIs (`Pinecone`,
`ServerlessSpec`), including revised index creation/ready-wait logic and
upsert/query usage.
> 
> Consolidates and guards notebook imports in an early try/except cell,
removes duplicated imports later, fixes a broken documentation link to
`guides/projects/understanding-projects`, and adds a final **Cleanup**
cell to delete the created index to avoid ongoing cost.

Bump urllib3 from 2.3.0 to 2.6.3 (#506) Bumps [urllib3](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3) from 2.3.0 to 2.6.3.
Release notes

Sourced from urllib3's releases.

2.6.3

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Changes

  • Fixed a security issue where decompression-bomb safeguards of the streaming API were bypassed when HTTP redirects were followed. (CVE-2026-21441 reported by @​D47A, 8.9 High, GHSA-38jv-5279-wg99)
  • Started treating Retry-After times greater than 6 hours as 6 hours by default. (urllib3/urllib3#3743)
  • Fixed urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection on Emscripten. (urllib3/urllib3#3752)

2.6.2

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Changes

  • Fixed HTTPResponse.read_chunked() to properly handle leftover data in the decoder's buffer when reading compressed chunked responses. (urllib3/urllib3#3734)

2.6.1

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Changes

  • Restore previously removed HTTPResponse.getheaders() and HTTPResponse.getheader() methods. (#3731)

2.6.0

🚀 urllib3 is fundraising for HTTP/2 support

urllib3 is raising ~$40,000 USD to release HTTP/2 support and ensure long-term sustainable maintenance of the project after a sharp decline in financial support. If your company or organization uses Python and would benefit from HTTP/2 support in Requests, pip, cloud SDKs, and thousands of other projects please consider contributing financially to ensure HTTP/2 support is developed sustainably and maintained for the long-haul.

Thank you for your support.

Security

  • Fixed a security issue where streaming API could improperly handle highly compressed HTTP content ("decompression bombs") leading to excessive resource consumption even when a small amount of data was requested. Reading small chunks of compressed data is safer and much more efficient now. (CVE-2025-66471 reported by @​Cycloctane, 8.9 High, GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37)
  • Fixed a security issue where an attacker could compose an HTTP response with virtually unlimited links in the Content-Encoding header, potentially leading to a denial of service (DoS) attack by exhausting system resources during decoding. The number of allowed chained encodings is now limited to 5. (CVE-2025-66418 reported by @​illia-v, 8.9 High, GHSA-gm62-xv2j-4w53)

[!IMPORTANT]

  • If urllib3 is not installed with the optional urllib3[brotli] extra, but your environment contains a Brotli/brotlicffi/brotlipy package anyway, make sure to upgrade it to at least Brotli 1.2.0 or brotlicffi 1.2.0.0 to benefit from the security fixes and avoid warnings. Prefer using urllib3[brotli] to install a compatible Brotli package automatically.

... (truncated)

  • Started treating Retry-After times greater than 6 hours as 6 hours by default. ([#3743](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3743) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3743>__)
  • Fixed urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection on Emscripten. ([#3752](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3752) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3752>__)

2.6.2 (2025-12-11)

2.6.1 (2025-12-08)

  • Restore previously removed HTTPResponse.getheaders() and HTTPResponse.getheader() methods. ([#3731](https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3731) <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/issues/3731>__)

2.6.0 (2025-12-05)

Security

  • Fixed a security issue where streaming API could improperly handle highly compressed HTTP content ("decompression bombs") leading to excessive resource consumption even when a small amount of data was requested. Reading small chunks of compressed data is safer and much more efficient now. (GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37 <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/security/advisories/GHSA-2xpw-w6gg-jr37>__)
  • Fixed a security issue where an attacker could compose an HTTP response with virtually unlimited links in the Content-Encoding header, potentially leading to a denial of service (DoS) attack by exhausting system resources during decoding. The number of allowed chained encodings is now limited to 5. (GHSA-gm62-xv2j-4w53 <https://github.com/urllib3/urllib3/security/advisories/GHSA-gm62-xv2j-4w53>__)

.. caution::

  • If urllib3 is not installed with the optional urllib3[brotli] extra, but your environment contains a Brotli/brotlicffi/brotlipy package anyway, make sure to upgrade it to at least Brotli 1.2.0 or brotlicffi 1.2.0.0 to benefit from the security fixes and avoid warnings. Prefer using

langchain-pinecone 0.2.13)

fix(docs): update langchain-retrieval-augmentation.ipynb to SDK v8 (#529)

## Summary

- Update Pinecone SDK to v8.0.0
- Pin langchain to 1.2.7 and add requests dependency
- Consolidate imports in first code cell with try/except wrapper
- Fix notebook JSON structure (missing 'name' and 'metadata' fields)
- Remove duplicate imports from later cells
- Format with ruff

This notebook is for developers who want to learn how to build a RAG
pipeline using Pinecone, LangChain, and OpenAI. It demonstrates
retrieval-augmented generation using Pinecone release notes as a
knowledge base.

## Prerequisites

- Pinecone API key (free tier available)
- OpenAI API key

## Key Concepts

- LangChain text splitters for document chunking
- OpenAI embeddings for vectorization
- Pinecone for vector storage and similarity search
- RAG to augment LLM responses with retrieved context

commit.message: Iterate on cursor commands

feat(ci): add notebook quality checks and pre-commit hooks (#527)

## Summary

This PR adds CI checks that enforce the notebook guidelines defined in
`.cursor/rules/` and catch common issues before review:

- **Pre-commit hooks**: nbstripout (strips notebook outputs) and
codespell (typo detection)
- **Secrets check**: Detects hardcoded API keys (OpenAI, Pinecone,
HuggingFace, AWS, etc.)
- **Structure check**: Validates notebooks have proper intro, grouped
imports, and cleanup cells
- **Timeless check**: Catches dated language like "as of 2024" or
"state-of-the-art"
- **Pinning check**: Requires version pins in pip install commands for
reproducibility

## Intended Audience

Notebook authors and reviewers who want automated enforcement of quality
guidelines.

## Key Concepts

All new checks run as separate jobs in the lint workflow on PRs. The
checks use poetry to install dependencies (nbformat) from the dev
dependency group.

## Test Plan

## Summary

This PR adds CI checks that enforce the notebook guidelines defined in
`.cursor/rules/` and catch common issues before review:

- **Pre-commit hooks**: nbstripout (strips notebook outputs) and
codespell (typo detection)
- **Secrets check**: Detects hardcoded API keys (OpenAI, Pinecone,
HuggingFace, AWS, etc.)
- **Structure check**: Validates notebooks have proper intro, grouped
imports, and cleanup cells
- **Timeless check**: Catches dated language like "as of 2024" or
"state-of-the-art"
- **Pinning check**: Requires version pins in pip install commands for
reproducibility

## Intended Audience

Notebook authors and reviewers who want automated enforcement of quality
guidelines.

All new checks run as separate jobs in the lint workflow on PRs. The
checks use poetry to install dependencies (nbformat) from the dev
dependency group.

## Summary

Updates the `docs/assistant-retrieve-context-snippets.ipynb` notebook to
use the latest Pinecone Python SDK and improves overall documentation
quality.

This notebook demonstrates how to use the Pinecone Assistant's `context`
operation to retrieve relevant information snippets for queries, and how
to combine this with an external LLM (Claude) for RAG-style
applications.

## Changes

- Update `pinecone` version from `7.0.2` to `~7.0` (latest v7 release)
- Add Colab and nbviewer badges to notebook header
- Fix API docs URL to use non-versioned path
- Fix section numbering (section 5 -> section 4)
- Fix typos in code comments and markdown
- Format code cells with ruff

## Intended Audience

Developers looking to use Pinecone Assistant's context retrieval
capabilities with their own LLM or to bypass the LLM reasoning step
while still benefiting from Assistant's parsing, chunking, and retrieval
features.

## Prerequisites

- Pinecone API key
- Anthropic API key (for the Claude integration section)

## Key Concepts Demonstrated

- Creating and managing Pinecone Assistants
- Uploading documents to Assistant
- Using the `context()` operation for direct snippet retrieval
- Combining context snippets with an external LLM (Claude)
- Validating results with Assistant's `chat()` operation

docs: update how-to-create-pinecone-datasets notebook to SDK v8 (#510) ## Summary Updates the Pinecone Datasets notebook to be compatible with Pinecone Python SDK v8. commit.message:
build: add ruff linting with pre-commit hooks and CI (#509)

## Summary

This PR adds automated code formatting and linting for Python files and
Jupyter notebooks using [ruff](https://docs.astral.sh/ruff/), replacing
the unused black dependency.

- **Pre-commit hooks**: Automatically format and lint code on commit
- **CI integration**: Lint workflow checks only changed files to avoid
reformatting legacy code
- **Contributor documentation**: New CONTRIBUTING.md with development
setup instructions

## Changes

- Replace `black` with `ruff` and `pre-commit` in pyproject.toml
- Add ruff configuration targeting notebooks with sensible defaults
(pycodestyle, pyflakes, isort)
- Create `.pre-commit-config.yaml` with ruff hooks
- Add `.github/workflows/lint.yaml` that runs on PRs for changed `.py`
and `.ipynb` files only
- Create `CONTRIBUTING.md` with setup instructions
- Update `README.md` to link to the new contributing guide

## Intended Audience

Contributors to the examples repository who want consistent code style
without manually running formatters.

## Key Concepts

- Incremental adoption: Only files that are touched get formatted,
avoiding large diffs from legacy code
- Local + CI enforcement: Pre-commit catches issues before push, CI
ensures nothing slips through

## Test Plan

- [ ] Verify pre-commit hooks work locally with `poetry install &&
poetry run pre-commit install`
- [ ] Confirm CI workflow runs on PR and checks only changed files
- [ ] Test that ruff correctly formats/lints notebook code cells

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk: this PR only adds developer tooling (ruff/pre-commit) and CI
checks, with no runtime code-path changes. The main risk is
CI/pre-commit friction or false positives due to lint/format rules and
the changed-files detection logic.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds **ruff-based linting/formatting** as the standard for this repo:
introduces `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, adds `ruff`/`pre-commit` to
`pyproject.toml` (removing `black`), and updates `poetry.lock`
accordingly.
> 
> Adds a reusable GitHub Actions `Lint` workflow that runs `ruff check`
and `ruff format --check` only on changed `.py`/`.ipynb` files, and
wires it into the main PR workflow.
> 
> Adds contributor-facing guidance by creating `CONTRIBUTING.md`,
updating `README.md` to point to it, and adding a `.cursor` voice/style
guide file.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
c11f31f4c550aad231b3cffecfa3ba4801dce522. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure

commit.message:
Pinned Numpy Version to avoid conflicts with Datasets in semantic-search notebook (#495)

## Problem

Describe the purpose of this change. What problem is being solved and
why?

An earlier change to the semantic search notebook broke functionality,
by causing a new version of numpy to be installed (2.3.2), which broke
the datasets library's install of tatoeba. This commit pins numpy to an
earlier version (2.0.2) that allows for the notebook to run as intended.

## Solution

Describe the approach you took. Link to any relevant bugs, issues, docs,
or other resources.

I tested between colab and local frequently, and found that colab's uv
instance has numpy 2.3.2, whereas their pip instance has 2.0.2. pip did
not experience this issue install when using datasets previously, so I
tested pinning the uv install of numpy to 2.0.2, which worked on:

- the colab instance
- an uploaded local instance to colab as well

commit.message:
Fix "pip" collision in workflow script causing normal cells to be filtered out (#489)

## Problem

Describe the purpose of this change. What problem is being solved and
why?

The convert-notebook script, used in the test notebooks action was
failing notebooks that should pass under normal circumstances. The issue
was a line in the workflow script that filtered out cells containing the
string "pip", which filtered out "pipeline", "pipe", etc. This can be
seen in the following prs:

Instance 1: "pipeline" getting filtered out:
https://github.com/pinecone-io/examples/pull/474
Instance 2: "pipe" getting filtered out:
https://github.com/pinecone-io/examples/actions/runs/16154448867/job/45593572999?pr=473

## Solution

Describe the approach you took. Link to any relevant bugs, issues, docs,
or other resources.

I added a list of pip commands we'd want to ignore at the relevant level
of preprocessing and had the script filter cells with those out, instead
of "pip"

commit.message:
Skip template strings when verifying links (#456)

## Problem

We don't want the link checker to fail when trying to verify links like
`https://reddit.com/r/{x['subreddit']}/comments/{x['id']}`

## Solution

- Skip links that contain `{`.
- Modifying the regex match to exclude `{` will just match on the first
segment `https://reddit.com/r/` which is not what we want.

## Type of Change

- [x] Infrastructure change (CI configs, etc)

commit.message:

## Problem

Describe the purpose of this change. What problem is being solved and
why?

The prior semantic search notebook was outdated and demonstrated many
patterns that are easier to do in Pinecone natively now. Additionally,
the notebook was lengthy and indexed a TON of vectors that took a while
to demonstrate. We need a faster notebook more in line with modern
practices with Pinecone.

## Solution

Describe the approach you took. Link to any relevant bugs, issues, docs,
or other resources.

- Replaced sentence transformers with Pinecone Integrated Inference
- Replaced lengthy dataset with a smaller, more instructive one. The
smaller dataset also can easily be modified for a larger subset if a
user desires
- Better API key handling for colab or local development
- Should be much faster in CI/CD/Github Actions too.

commit.message:
Add workflow to process changes after merge to master (#440)

## Problem

We would like to trigger some actions based on changes to this
repository

## Solution

Introduce a new workflow that is run whenever changes are merged to
`master` branch (the default branch). The workflow is defined in
`.github/workflows/post-merge.yaml` and uses git with `diff-filter`s to
group changes by whether they are additions, deletions, or
modifications. Then it invokes specific actions to handle each type of
change, although for now these actions are just stubs. More work will be
needed to implement the real contents of each action.

- The names of added notebooks are passed to the
`.github/actions/post-merge-additions/process-additions.py` script.
- The names of removed notebooks are passed to the
`.github/actions/post-merge-deletions/process-deletions.py` script
- The names of changed notebooks are passed to the
`.github/actions/post-merge-modifications/process-modifications.py`

For simplicity, renames are handled as deletion + addition, so when a
file is renamed both actions will be triggered.

## Next steps

Arjun should set an API key in the `ARJUN_PINECONE_API_KEY` repository
secret (referenced in since that is what is being fed through to the
actions as `PINECONE_API_KEY`. Currently I just stuck a placeholder
value in to verify the jobs and actions are wired up correctly, but
wasn't using Pinecone for anything yet inside these actions so there was
no need for an actual key.

For iterating on these scripts mentioned above, it's probably easiest to
run them locally and just poke in different values yourself via
environment variable, e.g. run something like this from the terminal:
`PINECONE_API_KEY='yourkey' NOTEBOOK='docs/it-threat-detection.ipynb'
.github/actions/post-merge-modifications/process-modifications.py` This
will allow you to iterate on the contents of the
`process-modifications.py` script without having to do a bunch of
unnecessary pushing and merging.

- [x] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)

functionality to not work as expected)

commit.message:
Fix Colab Widgets Error across ALL notebooks (#437)

## Problem

A subset of notebooks saved from Colab were not being loaded on Github
correctly. This was preventing people from seeing the notebook in the
browser, and subsequently, running them at all.
## Solution

Describe the approach you took. Link to any relevant bugs, issues, docs,
or other resources.

Using @jamescalam's fix and guidance from @jseldess, I wrote a script
that removed the widgets field, which resolves the issue without
modifying the behavior of the notebook too much. These extra fields are
created when you work on a notebook in colab, and download/save/upload
it.

See also:
https://gist.github.com/shacharmirkin/7f608c51f5d1c159d5c5791081eb5c6d

commit.message:
Overhaul article recommendation notebook (#432)

## Problem

Notebook has outdated dependencies was is in an unrunnable state

## Solution

- Update dependencies
- Remove wordcloud dependency which I wasn't able to install in python
3.12 in my jupyter environment due to [this
issue](https://github.com/amueller/word_cloud/issues/791)
- Refactor the example code to process CVS in chunks rather than loading
the entire file into memory at once as this was causing my jupyter
instance to crash even with the smaller dataset
- Migrate data file from Dropbox into a Google Cloud Storage Bucket. The
wget command that was there was failing to pull from Dropbox because
they want to nag people about signing up for their service before
letting you download.
- Also prepared a smaller subset of the data and stored that separately
so there's no longer a need to download the entire huge dataset (which
was taking me 30 minutes on my home connection) only to turn around and
discard 90% of the rows anyway.

readme_html:
Pinecone Examples

This repository is a collection of sample applications and Jupyter Notebooks that you can run, download, study and modify in order to get hands-on with Pinecone vector databases and common AI patterns, tools and algorithms.

Two kinds of examples

This repo contains:

  1. Production ready examples in ./docs that receive regular review and support from the Pinecone engineering team
  2. Examples optimized for learning and exploration of AI techniques in ./learn and patterns for building different kinds of applications, created and maintained by the Pinecone Developer Advocacy team.

We appreciate your feedback and contributions. Please see CONTRIBUTING.md for information on how to contribute to this repo.

Getting started

Please see our Getting started guide in our learn section for detailed instructions and a walkthrough of setting up and running a Jupyter Notebook in Google Colab for experimentation.

We love feedback!

As you work through these examples, if you encounter any problems or things that are confusing or don't work quite right, please open a new issue :octocat:.

Getting support and further reading

Visit our:

  • Documentation
  • Support forums

Contributing

See CONTRIBUTING.md for guidelines on how to contribute.
